qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthorized Order Access via Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR)
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes an IDOR vulnerability in order/cart retrieval endpoints (PaymentsController, CartController, DownloadsController). The advisory describes blind SQL injection in hasVariant/hasProduct properties bypassing a blocklist in ElementIndexesController. Different vulnerability class (IDOR vs SQL injection), different vulnerable code (order retrieval vs hasVariant/hasProduct subquery construction), different attack vector (order number enumeration vs SQL injection through criteria parameter), different affected components.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory (GHSA-r54v-qq87-px5r) describes blind SQL injection in Craft Commerce's hasVariant and hasProduct query properties that bypass a blocklist added to ElementIndexesController. The vulnerability involves passing attacker-controlled SQL through criteria[hasVariant][where] or criteria[hasProduct][where] parameters. The finding describes an IDOR vulnerability in order retrieval endpoints, which is unrelated to SQL injection, hasVariant/hasProduct query construction, Craft::configure() subquery handling, or the ElementIndexesController blocklist bypass mechanism.
evidence: Advisory describes blind SQL injection via hasVariant/hasProduct properties bypassing ElementIndexesController blocklist
Advisory requires authenticated control panel user (PR:L), affects craftcms/commerce package
IDOR finding describes unauthorized order access via predictable order numbers in PaymentsController/CartController (no SQL injection)
Finding does not reference hasVariant, hasProduct, ElementIndexesController, or SQL injection through criteria parameters
